en.extract:
The Zamboanga bulbul (Hypsipetes rufigularis) is a songbird species in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae. It is endemic to the Philippines, where its natural habitat is tropical moist lowland forests of Basilan and the Zamboanga Peninsula. It is becoming rare due to habitat loss.


Description and taxonomy

The Zamboanga bulbul was originally described in the genus Hypsipetes and later placed in the genus Ixos. Formerly, some authorities classified the Zamboanga bulbul in the genus Iole and also considered it to be a subspecies of the Philippine bulbul. In 2010, it was returned to the genus Hypsipetes. It is differentiated by its much larger size and lack of streaking on its throat. 


Ecology and behavior
Often observed visiting and feeding on fruiting trees. Pressumed to supplement diet with insects.
Nothing is known about its reproductive behaviour. 


Habitat and conservation status
Found in primary and mature secondary forest. It is most common in intact forest.
IUCN has assessed this bird as a near-threatened due to its smaller range. It is still relatively conmmon but forests in the Philippines have experienced rapid habitat destruction. 
Found in the protected area of Pasonanca Natural Park but still continues to experience encroachment.


Footnotes


References
Gregory, Steven M. (2000): Nomenclature of the Hypsipetes Bulbuls (Pycnonotidae). Forktail 16: 164–166. PDF fulltext
Moyle, Robert G. & Marks, Ben D. (2006): Phylogenetic relationships of the bulbuls (Aves: Pycnonotidae) based on mitochondrial and nuclear DNA sequence data. Mol. Phylogenet. Evol. 40(3): 687–695. doi:10.1016/j.ympev.2006.04.015 (HTML abstract)
Pasquet, Éric; Han, Lian-Xian; Khobkhet, Obhas & Cibois, Alice (2001): Towards a molecular systematics of the genus Criniger, and a preliminary phylogeny of the bulbuls (Aves, Passeriformes, Pycnonotidae). Zoosystema 23(4): 857–863. PDF fulltext

The '''Zamboanga bulbul''' ('''''Hypsipetes rufigularis''''') is a [[songbird]] [[species]] in the [[bulbul]] [[Family (taxonomy)|family]], Pycnonotidae. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to the Philippines, where its natural [[habitat]] is tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s of [[Basilan]] and the [[Zamboanga Peninsula (geographical region)|Zamboanga Peninsula]]. It is becoming rare due to [[habitat loss]].<ref>BLI (2008)</ref>

== Description and taxonomy ==
[[File:IoleRufigularisKeulemans.jpg|left|thumb|Illustration by [[John Gerrard Keulemans|Keulemans]], 1881]]
The Zamboanga bulbul was originally described in the genus ''Hypsipetes'' and later placed in the genus ''[[Ixos]]''. Formerly, some authorities classified the Zamboanga bulbul in the genus [[Iole (genus)|''Iole'']] and also considered it to be a subspecies of the [[Philippine bulbul]]. In 2010, it was returned to the genus ''Hypsipetes''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Species Version 2 « IOC World Bird List |url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/updates/archives/species-2-0-2-11/ |access-date=2017-11-03 |website=www.worldbirdnames.org |language=en-US}}</ref> It is differentiated by its much larger size and lack of streaking on its throat. 

== Ecology and behavior ==
Often observed visiting and feeding on fruiting trees. Pressumed to supplement diet with insects.

Nothing is known about its reproductive behaviour.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Fishpool |first1=Lincoln |last2=Tobias |first2=Joseph A. |date=2020 |title=Zamboanga Bulbul (Hypsipetes rufigularis), version 1.0 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/zambul1/1.0/introduction |journal=Birds of the World |language=en |doi=10.2173/bow.zambul1.01 |issn=2771-3105|url-access=subscription }}</ref> 

== Habitat and conservation status ==
Found in primary and mature secondary forest. It is most common in intact forest.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Fishpool |first1=Lincoln |last2=Tobias |first2=Joseph A. |date=2020 |title=Zamboanga Bulbul (Hypsipetes rufigularis), version 1.0 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/zambul1/1.0/introduction |journal=Birds of the World |language=en |doi=10.2173/bow.zambul1.01 |issn=2771-3105|url-access=subscription }}</ref>

IUCN has assessed this bird as a [[Near-threatened species|near-threatened]] due to its smaller range. It is still relatively conmmon but forests in the [[Philippines]] have experienced rapid habitat destruction. 

Found in the protected area of [[Pasonanca Natural Park]] but still continues to experience encroachment.<ref>{{Cite report |url=https://www.iucnredlist.org/species/22713182/131968765 |title=Hypsipetes rufigularis: BirdLife International: The IUCN Red List of Threatened Species 2018: e.T22713182A131968765 |last=IUCN |date=2018-08-09 |publisher=International Union for Conservation of Nature |doi=10.2305/iucn.uk.2018-2.rlts.t22713182a131968765.en |language=en}}</ref>

''[[Molecular Phylogenetics and Evolution|Mol. Phylogenet. Evol.]]'' 40(3): 687–695. <small>{{doi|10.1016/j.ympev.2006.04.015}}</small> (HTML abstract)

''Zoosystema'' 23(4): 857–863. [http://www.mnhn.fr/publication/zoosyst/z01n4a12.pdf PDF fulltext]
